canonical_files_verified.ticket_title: BA Specification: Close /allow grant leakage (D) and PostToolUse:Agent tracker race (H) — iter3
canonical_files_verified.context_requirement_clarified_prefix: Land code-fix items D + H from the prior Codex triage of leftover work after cycle 20260519-151734 closed.
canonical_files_verified.verification_method: Read both canonical files in full; cross-referenced title and requirement.clarified prefix against dispatch prompt expectations.

summary: iter3 FINAL: OBJ-4 and OBJ-5 cleanly resolved (BLOCKER-level dimension fixes complete). OBJ-6 remains UNRESOLVED with two concrete defects in the canonical context JSON: (a) NO `iteration_log[]` field exists anywhere in context-20260519-211515.json — the dispatch prompt explicitly required `iteration_log[] has iter2 entry`, and this field is entirely absent. (b) `collision_report_recurring.second_collision.preserved_paths` (lines 104-106) still references `-DH` suffixed paths (docs/dev/ticket-20260519-211515-DH.md, docs/dev/context-20260519-211515-DH.json) that DO NOT EXIST on disk — on-disk state shows only `-CYCLE2` and `-CYCLE2-round2` suffixed ghost files, with D+H content promoted to the canonical 20260519-211515 slots. The dispatch prompt explicitly required `collision_report.preserved_artifact_paths[] reflects on-disk state (-CYCLE2 suffix, not -DH)`. BA failed to update both fields. Additionally, Codex's prior ISSUE-1 (test-writer contract gap) was verified by direct read of commands/dev.md:606-630 and agents/test-writer.md — the gap is REAL, not forward-looking: STANDARD tier dispatches test-writer expecting non-null acceptance_criteria_path, but BA set it to null per dispatch instruction. Codex iter3 adversarial review independently arrived at FAIL with the same evidence. Per dev.md max-3 BA-QA loop ceiling, this is the final iteration; orchestrator will proceed with best-effort BA output but MUST resolve OBJ-6 metadata defects and Codex ISSUE-1 contract gap before Dev dispatch (either regenerate companion AC JSON from the 10 D+H ACs, OR downgrade complexity_tier to SMALL to bypass test-writer dispatch, AND patch the iteration_log + preserved_paths fields).

evidence_quality.notes: AC-H3a/H3b line citations verified end-to-end against ticket lines 130-150. Helper specification verified against ticket lines 175-198 with embedded Python reference implementation. Codex consultation succeeded via tee-less channel; codex returned independent FAIL verdict with same OBJ-6 evidence. Direct file-listing confirmed -DH files do not exist on disk.

scope_alignment.notes: BA iter3 did not narrow or expand scope. D+H remain the only fix items. Out-of-scope items (A/B/C/E/F/G) remain excluded. The user verbatim '好的开完成全部' is preserved in context line 11 and ticket line 224.

investigation_completeness.notes: BA failed to satisfy the dispatch prompt's explicit OBJ-6 requirements: (1) `iteration_log[] has iter2 entry` — field entirely absent from context JSON. (2) `collision_report.preserved_artifact_paths[] reflects on-disk state (-CYCLE2 suffix, not -DH)` — field still references non-existent -DH paths. BA's iter3_objections_resolved.OBJ-6.resolution text acknowledges the gap as 'partially_resolved_with_trade_off_surfaced' but does not address the iteration_log or preserved_paths defects at all.

affected_file_accuracy.notes: Context JSON `collision_report_recurring.second_collision.preserved_paths` (lines 104-106) lists `docs/dev/ticket-20260519-211515-DH.md` and `docs/dev/context-20260519-211515-DH.json` — verified by direct `ls -la` that neither file exists on disk. On-disk reality (from ls output): only ticket-20260519-211515-CYCLE2.md, ticket-20260519-211515-CYCLE2-round2.md, context-20260519-211515-CYCLE2.json, context-20260519-211515-CYCLE2-round2.json ghost files remain — D+H content is now at the canonical -211515.md and -211515.json paths. The preserved_paths field is therefore inaccurate and misleading for any downstream auditor.

spec_text_vs_execution_drift.notes: OBJ-4 (AC-H3 GIVEN/THEN contradiction) and OBJ-5 (K=3 window unsafe when ip_index=None) are BOTH cleanly resolved. AC-H3a and AC-H3b are mutually-exclusive sub-ACs with no contradictory GIVEN/THEN clauses. Helper specification at ticket lines 177-193 unambiguously says `if ip_index is None: return None` with explicit comment 'There is NO `else: start = 0` fallback'. Edge Cases & Risks section line 85 explicitly notes 'Case A is gated on a single valid in_progress anchor.'

OBJ-4.evidence: Ticket lines 130-150: AC-H3 cleanly split into AC-H3a (subagent_type absent + legacy resolves single valid in_progress with subagent_call metadata → Case B legacy write allowed) and AC-H3b (4 enumerated no-write orphan cases). AC-H3a includes 'Why this AC exists' explanation tying back to AC-H2 serial dispatch. AC-H3b enumerates exactly the 4 cases per dispatch prompt: (1) subagent_type absent + legacy None; (2) subagent_type non-empty + typed None; (3) subagent_type non-empty + no in_progress; (4) subagent_type non-empty + multi-in_progress. No general 'subagent_calls UNCHANGED' THEN with 'missing subagent_type in stdin' GIVEN remains anywhere in the ticket.
OBJ-4.verification_method: Direct grep + line-citation read of AC-H3a/H3b sections; cross-referenced against AC-H2 serial-dispatch description at lines 124-128.

OBJ-5.evidence: Ticket lines 175-198: helper `_find_earliest_unflipped_matching_step` rewritten with concrete Python reference implementation (lines 179-193). Critical lines: line 183-184 `if ip_index is None: return None`. Line 177 explicitly states 'There is NO `else: start = 0` fallback'. Line 194 mandates multi-in_progress guard at `_current_in_progress_index` entry: 'if more than one in_progress step is found, return None'. Edge Cases & Risks section line 85 documents 'Case A is gated on a single valid in_progress anchor.' Case A → AC-H3b no-write fall-through documented at line 85.
OBJ-5.verification_method: Direct line-cited read of Technical Hints H section and Edge Cases & Risks H section.

OBJ-6.evidence: TWO defects remain in context-20260519-211515.json: (a) NO `iteration_log[]` field exists anywhere in the file (grep '^.*iteration_log' returns no top-level match) — the dispatch prompt explicitly required iter2 entry. (b) `collision_report_recurring.second_collision.preserved_paths` at lines 104-106 lists `docs/dev/ticket-20260519-211515-DH.md` and `docs/dev/context-20260519-211515-DH.json`. Direct `ls -la /dev/shm/dev-workspace/dot-claude/docs/dev/` confirms neither file exists on disk. On-disk state shows only -CYCLE2 / -CYCLE2-round2 suffixed ghost files; D+H content has been promoted to the canonical 20260519-211515 slots. BA's iter3_objections_resolved.OBJ-6.resolution acknowledges only the `acceptance_criteria_path: null` and `_companion_artifacts_note` aspects of OBJ-6 — does not mention iteration_log or preserved_paths.
OBJ-6.partial_credit: BA did satisfy the `acceptance_criteria_path: null` and `blast_radius_map_path: null` portions of OBJ-6, and added `_companion_artifacts_note` explanation. But the iteration_log and preserved_paths sub-requirements were missed entirely.
OBJ-6.verification_method: Direct grep of context JSON for 'iteration_log'; direct `ls -la` of docs/dev/ directory verifying -DH file non-existence.

codex_consult.channel: Skill(skill='codex') with gpt-5.5 xhigh reasoning — output saved to docs/dev/qa-output-20260519-211515-iter3-codex.txt
codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex via Skill protocol independently returned FAIL with structured per-issue verdict table. OBJ-4 RESOLVED (OBSERVATION_ONLY): AC-H3a/H3b cleanly separated in ticket-20260519-211515.md:130-150. OBJ-5 RESOLVED (OBSERVATION_ONLY): helper spec says 'if ip_index is None: return None' and forbids 'else: start=0' in ticket-20260519-211515.md:177-194. OBJ-6a UNRESOLVED: missing iteration_log[] — PROPOSED_FIX: add top-level iteration_log to context-20260519-211515.json with iter2 entry referencing ba-qa-report-20260519-211515-iter2.json. OBJ-6b UNRESOLVED: stale -DH preserved_paths — PROPOSED_FIX: replace context lines 104-107 with actual on-disk -CYCLE2 artifacts. ISSUE-1 REAL BLOCKER: complexity_tier=STANDARD + acceptance_criteria_path=null violates test-writer contract — PROPOSED_FIX: prefer R1 (regenerate docs/dev/acceptance-criteria-20260519-211515.json from 10 D+H ACs and set acceptance_criteria_path non-null); R2 downgrade to SMALL is weaker and should only be used if explicitly justified, because it bypasses test-writer rather than satisfying the STANDARD-tier contract. Codex final: 'FAIL is correct. Do not PASS just because OBJ-4/OBJ-5 blockers are clean. Since iter3 is final, do not loop BA again. Escalate to orchestrator-side deterministic artifact repair before Dev dispatch.'
codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Codex's structured per-issue verdict table confirmed my preliminary FAIL stance with PROPOSED_FIX guidance for each unresolved issue. Codex explicitly preferred R1 (regenerate AC JSON) over R2 (downgrade tier) because R1 satisfies the STANDARD-tier contract rather than bypassing it — I've updated the downstream_blockers_for_dev_dispatch resolution_options ordering to reflect codex's preference. Codex confirmed that 'compliant-but-non-dispatchable context is still a FAIL/BLOCKED outcome' and that the 'dispatch told BA to set null' argument changes blame attribution but not the verdict.

issue_1_test_writer_contract_gap.verification_evidence: Direct read of /root/.claude/commands/dev.md line 606 confirms 'If complexity_tier ∈ {STANDARD, COMPLEX} OR risk_level == high, then test_writer_expected = true'. Lines 620-623 of dispatch template specify 'acceptance_criteria_path: docs/dev/acceptance-criteria-<task_id>.json'. The test-writer dispatch step EXISTS in the current workflow for STANDARD tier. Context complexity_tier='STANDARD' (line 7), so test-writer WILL be dispatched, but acceptance_criteria_path is null — contract violation.
issue_1_test_writer_contract_gap.qa_recommendation: Orchestrator MUST choose between: (R1) regenerate docs/dev/acceptance-criteria-20260519-211515.json from the 10 D+H ACs in ticket (currently file at that path contains stale Cycle 2 content per OBJ-6 iter2 evidence — must be overwritten); OR (R2) downgrade context complexity_tier to SMALL (matches actual scope of 2 hooks + 1 test file, ≤200 LOC).

objections.claim: Context-20260519-211515.json is missing the top-level `iteration_log[]` field that the dispatch prompt explicitly required to contain an iter2 entry. Direct grep of the file returns no top-level `iteration_log` key. BA's iter3 narrative talks about the iter1/iter2 history in the collision_report_recurring section and in iter3_objections_resolved, but does not produce the structured iteration_log[] array.
objections.problem: Downstream auditors and the close-cycle report inspector expect a structured iteration_log[] entry per BA-QA iteration so the cycle's revision history is queryable. The current context is missing this entirely. The dispatch prompt for iter3 explicitly listed 'iteration_log[] has iter2 entry (the iter that just landed; counting from iter1 as the first revision)' as part of OBJ-6 resolution criteria.
objections.required_evidence: Orchestrator MUST add an iteration_log[] array to context-20260519-211515.json with at least an iter2 entry (the BA iter that landed before iter3 began), including iter1 if backfill is desired. Example shape: [{'iter': 1, 'resolved': ['OBJ-1'], 'introduced': ['OBJ-4', 'OBJ-5', 'OBJ-6']}, {'iter': 2, 'resolved': [], 'introduced': []}].

objections.claim: Context-20260519-211515.json `collision_report_recurring.second_collision.preserved_paths` (lines 104-106) lists `docs/dev/ticket-20260519-211515-DH.md` and `docs/dev/context-20260519-211515-DH.json`. Direct `ls -la /dev/shm/dev-workspace/dot-claude/docs/dev/` confirms neither file exists on disk. On-disk reality: only `-CYCLE2` and `-CYCLE2-round2` ghost files exist; D+H content has been promoted by the orchestrator to the canonical `-211515.md` and `-211515.json` paths.
objections.problem: The preserved_paths field is misleading. Any downstream auditor following these paths will fail to find the artifacts, and may falsely conclude the D+H content was lost. The dispatch prompt for iter3 explicitly required `collision_report.preserved_artifact_paths[] reflects on-disk state (-CYCLE2 suffix, not -DH)`.
objections.required_evidence: Orchestrator MUST update collision_report_recurring.second_collision.preserved_paths to either (a) reference the actual on-disk -CYCLE2 ghost paths (ticket-20260519-211515-CYCLE2.md, ticket-20260519-211515-CYCLE2-round2.md, context-20260519-211515-CYCLE2.json, context-20260519-211515-CYCLE2-round2.json) which are the PARALLEL cycle's artifacts, OR (b) note that the second collision was resolved by orchestrator-promoting D+H content to canonical slots (no -DH artifacts persist), with the parallel cycle's content quarantined under -CYCLE2 suffix.

objections.claim: Context-20260519-211515.json `complexity_tier: "STANDARD"` (line 7) + `acceptance_criteria_path: null` (line 55) violates the /dev Step 8 test-writer dispatch contract. Direct read of commands/dev.md confirms: line 606-610 dispatches test-writer when tier ∈ {STANDARD, COMPLEX} OR risk_level == high; lines 620-623 specify the dispatch prompt passes `acceptance_criteria_path: docs/dev/acceptance-criteria-<task_id>.json`; agents/test-writer.md lines 24-29 and 178 require this path to be non-null. BA's `_companion_artifacts_note` (line 57) acknowledges this gap but does not resolve it.
objections.problem: Downstream Dev dispatch WILL fail or load stale Cycle 2 content. If orchestrator dispatches test-writer with null path, test-writer aborts. If orchestrator uses the existing on-disk file at docs/dev/acceptance-criteria-20260519-211515.json, that file contains Cycle 2 ACs (verified per iter2 QA report OBJ-6 evidence), not D+H ACs, so test-writer generates tests for the wrong scope.
objections.required_evidence: Orchestrator MUST choose between: (R1) overwrite docs/dev/acceptance-criteria-20260519-211515.json with D+H ACs derived from the 10 ACs in the ticket (AC-D1, AC-D2, AC-D3, AC-D4, AC-H1, AC-H2, AC-H3a, AC-H3b, AC-H4, AC-D-H), and update context line 55 to point to it; OR (R2) downgrade context complexity_tier to SMALL, which bypasses the test-writer dispatch gate per commands/dev.md:608 (SMALL/MICRO tiers do not trigger test_writer_expected=true).

AC-H3b enumerates exactly the 4 no-write cases (subagent_type absent+legacy None; subagent_type non-empty+typed None; subagent_type non-empty+no in_progress; subagent_type non-empty+multi-in_progress).

No `else: start = 0` branch. Multi-in_progress guard mandated at line 194. Edge Cases & Risks line 85 documents single-anchor gating.

BA DID NOT add `iteration_log[]` with iter2 entry. BA DID NOT update `collision_report.preserved_paths` from -DH to -CYCLE2 on-disk reality. Plus the null acceptance_criteria_path violates the downstream test-writer contract (OBJ-9).

out_of_scope_observations.observation: Per dev.md max-3 BA-QA loop ceiling, iter3 is the FINAL BA iteration. Orchestrator MUST proceed with best-effort BA output but resolve the 3 OBJ-7/OBJ-8/OBJ-9 metadata + contract defects pre-Dev-dispatch via direct orchestrator-side edits (the BA agent has no more iteration budget to fix them).

The recurring task-id 20260519-211515 collision between this D+H cycle and the parallel Cycle 2 of spec-20260518-225715 is an orchestrator-side allocator bug (second collision in same slot). Out of scope for the current cycle's D+H validation but warrants a separate orchestrator cleanup ticket. BA documented this in collision_report_recurring.orchestrator_required_resolution[1].

self_verification.evidence_provenance: Direct full-file reads of ticket-20260519-211515.md (236 lines) and context-20260519-211515.json (116 lines). Direct grep for iteration_log key (returned no matches). Direct ls -la of docs/dev/ confirming -DH file non-existence and -CYCLE2 file existence. Direct read of commands/dev.md:606-634 confirming test-writer dispatch for STANDARD tier. Codex independent adversarial review returned same FAIL verdict with same evidence.

verdict_rationale: FAIL. OBJ-4 (BLOCKER) and OBJ-5 (BLOCKER) are both cleanly resolved by BA iter3. OBJ-6 (MAJOR) is partially resolved — null acceptance_criteria_path + null blast_radius_map_path + _companion_artifacts_note explanation are in place, but iteration_log[] with iter2 entry is entirely absent, and preserved_paths still references non-existent -DH files. Additionally, the BA codex_consult ISSUE-1 (test-writer contract gap) is independently verified as a real blocker for Dev dispatch — commands/dev.md:606-630 unambiguously dispatches test-writer for STANDARD tier with non-null AC path expected. Per iter3 dispatch instructions, since this is the FINAL BA iteration, the verdict is FAIL but with explicit guidance that the orchestrator can resolve OBJ-7/OBJ-8/OBJ-9 via direct orchestrator-side edits to context-20260519-211515.json (no further BA work needed): either regenerate companion AC JSON or downgrade tier to SMALL, and patch the metadata fields.

resolution_options_in_codex_preferred_or: R1 (codex-preferred): Overwrite docs/dev/acceptance-criteria-20260519-211515.json with D+H ACs (from 10 ACs in ticket: AC-D1, AC-D2, AC-D3, AC-D4, AC-H1, AC-H2, AC-H3a, AC-H3b, AC-H4, AC-D-H); update context.acceptance_criteria_path to point to it. Satisfies the STANDARD-tier contract rather than bypassing it.
R2 (weaker, codex-deprecated): Downgrade context.complexity_tier to SMALL (bypasses test-writer dispatch). Should only be used if explicitly justified.
